teamObjective.explanation: My hand is all low non-trump clubs (3-6), so I cannot realistically dominate the trick. The best team play is to lead the weakest card and let my partner, who still has 3 cards, try to take control.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2, 3 cards) is likely to hold at least one card above club-3 and possibly a trump or high non-trump, since they have a normal starting count and no public cards have been played. They are in position to overtake a low lead.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards (tagged as near finish), so they may be saving strong cards to close the round and will try to win any low lead if they can. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards and also may hold higher cards. Neither opponent has revealed any cards.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of clubs is the lowest, least committal card. It maximizes the chance that partner can play a higher card or trump to take the lead, while preserving my 4-6 for future tricks if I regain control.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 6 is my highest card but still very weak in the absolute order. Playing it wastes my only card with decent local strength and is still likely beaten by an opponent or partner's higher card, leaving me with only 3-5.
Leading the 5 is only marginally stronger than the 3 and still unlikely to win the trick outright. Keeping the 5 gives me more flexibility for later plays.
The 4 is essentially the same category as the 3; leading the 3 is strictly lower and therefore gives partner one more rank of room to take the trick without extra risk.

risks: An opponent may hold a higher non-trump or trump and win the trick, possibly handing the lead to the near-finishing opponent (player 1).
Partner may not be able to beat the trick if their 3 cards are also low and non-trump.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the absolute lowest card, I minimize the value lost and do not telegraph strength, giving partner the widest possible margin to overtake or for opponents to spend a higher card.

notes: No partner signals exist yet because only new_game has occurred; all beliefs are probabilistic and grounded in hand counts and scenario tags.
